seasonArc: Lotte Kopecky underlined her status as the most complete one-day racer in the women's peloton across the 2026 spring. After a win at Nokere Koerse earlier in the week, she delivered the headline result of her early season at Milano-Sanremo Donne, reading the chaotic race perfectly: distanced briefly on the Cipressa, she fought back, made the decisive Poggio selection off Puck Pieterse's move, then won the five-up sprint on the Via Roma for another Monument. The cobbled classics didn't reward her in kind — fourth at the Ronde van Vlaanderen (+1:04) and fourth at Paris-Roubaix Femmes (+1:30) left her just off the podium in both — but a Monument win and back-to-back top-fours in the biggest cobbled races confirmed a spring of relentless front-of-race racing.
